---
title: "Adapter Framework"
subtitle: "Design rationale, architectural invariants, and the DatabaseAdapterBase contract"
uiao:
  id: UIAO_ADAPTER_FW
  status: Current
  owner: Governance Board
  tags: [adapter, architecture, governance]
  created: "2026-05-06"
  lastUpdated: "2026-05-06"
summary: >
  Reference documentation for the UIAO adapter framework. Covers the two-axis
  classification system, the DatabaseAdapterBase seven-domain contract, the
  Adapter Plane's execution environment, and the architectural invariants all
  adapters must satisfy.
fabric: adapter
---

# Adapter Framework

The UIAO adapter framework defines how external systems connect to the governance pipeline.
An adapter is a **read-only, stateless connector** that pulls data from an external system,
normalizes it into canonical claims, and emits evidence the governance pipeline consumes.
Adapters never write to source systems unless they are explicitly classed as `modernization`
adapters — and even then, only within well-defined boundaries.

## Classification axes

Every adapter is classified on two independent axes:

| Axis | Values | Meaning |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Operational class** | `conformance` / `modernization` | Does the adapter mutate the target system? |
| **Mission class** | `identity` / `telemetry` / `policy` / `enforcement` / `integration` | What governance domain does it serve? |

`conformance` adapters are the default. They live in `src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml`
and must never mutate. `modernization` adapters live in `src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml`
and may mutate within the invariants described in that file's preamble.

**Choosing mission class:**

| If your adapter reads... | mission class |
|--------------------------|--------------|
| Security posture, policy compliance | `policy` |
| Directory objects, identity records | `identity` |
| Metrics, logs, signals | `telemetry` |
| Enforcement decisions, action records | `enforcement` |
| Cross-system integration state | `integration` |

## The DatabaseAdapterBase contract

All adapters subclass `DatabaseAdapterBase` (`src/uiao/adapters/database_base.py`) and implement
seven canonical responsibility domains (per UIAO_121):

| # | Domain | Method | Returns |
|---|--------|--------|---------|
| 2.1 | Connection & Identity | `connect()` | `ConnectionProvenance` |
| 2.2 | Schema Discovery & Mapping | `discover_schema()` | `SchemaMappingObject` |
| 2.3 | Query Normalization | `execute_query(q)` | `QueryProvenance` |
| 2.4 | Data Normalization | `normalize(rows)` | `ClaimSet` |
| 2.5 | Drift Detection | `detect_drift()` | `DriftReport` |
| 2.6 | Evidence Packaging | `collect_evidence(ksi_id)` | `EvidenceObject` |
| 2.7 | Security / Operations | `__init__(config)` + static checks | — |

These dataclasses are frozen contracts. Any change to their shape requires an ADR.

**Domain 2.1 — Connection & Identity** establishes the provenance chain. `identity` must be unique
per connection attempt; `auth_method` must be non-empty. This is where credentials are validated
and TLS properties are recorded.

**Domain 2.2 — Schema Discovery** documents the translation between the vendor's field names and
UIAO's canonical shape. The `version_hash` must be deterministic — the conformance suite tests this
explicitly. `unmapped_fields` records vendor fields intentionally excluded (audit trail).

**Domain 2.3 — Query Normalization** translates canonical queries into vendor-specific filters.
The `execution_plan_hash` enables drift detection between query plan versions.

**Domain 2.4 — Data Normalization** is the adapter's core work. Every `ClaimObject` carries a
stable identity, control mapping, implementation status, and a `record_hash` for provenance.

**Domain 2.5 — Drift Detection** reports the adapter's own internal consistency assessment —
schema version drift, baseline divergence, or structural changes in the source system.

**Domain 2.6 — Evidence Packaging** is optional but strongly recommended. It lets the orchestrator
pull evidence for a specific Key Security Indicator by `ksi_id`.

**Domain 2.7 — Security / Operations** validates configuration at construction time. Reject unsafe
configs early; never defer to runtime.

## Four architectural invariants

All adapters must satisfy these invariants regardless of operational or mission class:

**Idempotency** — All adapter operations must be idempotent. Applying the same operation multiple
times produces the same result as applying it once. This is enforced by the conformance test suite.

**Statelessness** — Adapters must be stateless. All state is maintained by the Truth Fabric.
An adapter may not retain state between requests. The `_records` pattern in the tutorial is
only valid within a single invocation.

**Schema conformance** — All inputs and outputs must conform to the canonical schema registered
in the Truth Fabric. The `version_hash` on `SchemaMappingObject` is the mechanism for detecting
schema drift.

**Isolation** — Adapters operate within sandboxes with explicit resource limits (see ADR-017).
No adapter may access the resources of another adapter. The Adapter Plane enforces this
at the execution boundary.

## Adapter Plane — execution environment

The Adapter Plane (documented in [Appendix A](appendix/a-adapter-plane/index.qmd)) provides
four services to every registered adapter:

**Lifecycle management** — registration, authorization, suspension, and retirement.
The lifecycle state machine is defined in [A-01](appendix/a-adapter-plane/a-01-adapter-lifecycle.qmd).

**Sandbox execution** — isolated environments that prevent cross-adapter contamination and
unauthorized resource access. The isolation model is defined in
[A-02](appendix/a-adapter-plane/a-02-adapter-sandbox-execution.qmd) and ADR-017.

**Hot-swap and rollback** — zero-downtime adapter version upgrades with automatic rollback
on anomaly detection. The protocol is in
[A-03](appendix/a-adapter-plane/a-03-adapter-hot-swap-rollback.qmd) and ADR-021.

**Health and liveness monitoring** — continuous monitoring with automatic lifecycle actions
on degradation. Defined in
[A-04](appendix/a-adapter-plane/a-04-adapter-health-liveness.qmd) and ADR-025.

## File layout convention

Single-file adapters live directly under `src/uiao/adapters/`:

```
src/uiao/adapters/
├── bluecat_adapter.py
├── cyberark_adapter.py
├── scubagear_adapter.py
└── terraform_adapter.py
```

Adapters with heavy supporting machinery (IR transforms, runtime harnesses, schemas)
get a subdirectory beside the single-file entry point:

```
src/uiao/adapters/
├── scubagear_adapter.py       ← the adapter class
└── scuba/
    ├── ir/                    ← vendor-to-IR transformer
    ├── runtime/               ← runtime harness
    └── schemas/               ← normalized-JSON schema
```

Rule of thumb: start as a single file. Grow a subdirectory only when the adapter has
three or more supporting Python modules, a runtime, or a schema.

## Registry architecture

Two registries, one adapter each:

**`src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml`** — conformance adapters (read-only).
Required fields: `id`, `name`, `class`, `mission-class`, `status`, `phase`, `vendor`,
`license`, `runtime`, `runner-class`, `tenancy`, `scope`, `outputs`, `triggers`.
Schema: `src/uiao/schemas/adapter-registry/adapter-registry.schema.json`.

**`src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml`** — modernization adapters (may mutate).
Additional invariants documented in the registry file's preamble. Use only for adapters
that genuinely mutate the target system.

Validate any registry change before committing:

```bash
python -m pytest tests/test_adapter_registry.py -q
uiao substrate walk
```

## Related ADRs

| ADR | Scope |
|-----|-------|
| ADR-007 | Multi-cloud adapter model |
| ADR-013 | Adapter failure isolation |
| ADR-015 | Adapter extensibility |
| ADR-017 | Adapter sandbox execution |
| ADR-021 | Adapter hot-swap and rollback |
| ADR-023 | Adapter concurrency |
| ADR-025 | Adapter health and liveness |
| ADR-027 | Adapter retirement |
| ADR-049 | Microsoft adapter coverage expansion |

## See also

- [Adapter Authoring Tutorial](adapter-authoring-tutorial.qmd) — 30-minute walkthrough from zero to merged PR
- [Appendix A — Adapter Plane](appendix/a-adapter-plane/index.qmd) — full Adapter Plane specification
- [ADR Index](adr/index.qmd) — all binding decisions
